seoTitle: Sunshine Bay vs Suntop — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Sunshine Bay and Suntop. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Sunshine Bay vs Suntop: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Sunshine Bay and Suntop.
heading: Sunshine Bay vs Suntop — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Sunshine Bay, New South Wales and Suntop, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Sunshine Bay has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Suntop (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Sunshine Bay or Suntop safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Sunshine Bay has a flood risk score of 56/100, while Suntop scores 42/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Sunshine Bay scores 42/100 for bushfire risk. Suntop scores 59/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Sunshine Bay or Suntop?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Sunshine Bay has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.78% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
